items.headline: Anthropic's powerful Fable 5 model is switched back on worldwide
items.summary: On July 1, Anthropic brought Claude Fable 5 — one of its most capable models — back online for everyone, after the U.S. government lifted the export restrictions it had placed on the model in mid-June. It's available again across Claude.ai, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork, and is being re-enabled on the Amazon, Google, and Microsoft cloud platforms.
items.why_it_matters: If you'd lost access to a top-tier Claude model over the past few weeks, it's back — and the whole episode is a reminder not to build your business around a single AI tool, since access can shift fast when regulators get involved.

items.headline: Anthropic explains the safety guardrails it built before bringing Fable 5 back
items.summary: Anthropic detailed the fix behind Fable 5's return: a new safety 'classifier' (an AI filter that spots and blocks dangerous requests) that it says stops the specific misuse technique regulators were worried about in over 99% of cases. It also proposed a shared industry scorecard — the Cyber Jailbreak Severity scale, built with Amazon, Microsoft, and Google — for rating how serious an AI 'jailbreak' (a trick that gets around an AI's safety rules) actually is.
items.why_it_matters: It's a rare plain-language look at how AI companies try to make powerful tools safe enough to keep online — useful context if you've ever wondered whether the AI you depend on could suddenly be pulled over safety concerns.

categories.title: Tools & Tips for Creators and Small Biz
